Package neqsim.process.equipment.expander

  • Field Details

    • serialVersionUID

      private static final long serialVersionUID

      • Constant Field Values

    • expanderMechanicalDesign

      private ExpanderMechanicalDesign expanderMechanicalDesign
      Mechanical design for the expander.

    • ratedRecoveredPower

      private double ratedRecoveredPower
      Rated recovered shaft power of the expander in kW. A value of 0 (the default) means no recovered-power capacity limit is defined, so no recoveredPower constraint is created.

    • nozzleOpening

      private double nozzleOpening
      Current inlet guide vane or nozzle opening fraction.

    • targetNozzleOpening

      private double targetNozzleOpening
      Target inlet guide vane or nozzle opening fraction.

    • minimumNozzleOpening

      private double minimumNozzleOpening
      Minimum allowed inlet guide vane or nozzle opening fraction.

    • maximumNozzleOpening

      private double maximumNozzleOpening
      Maximum allowed inlet guide vane or nozzle opening fraction.

    • nozzleOpeningRate

      private double nozzleOpeningRate
      Maximum absolute inlet guide vane or nozzle stroke rate in fraction per second.

    • dynamicRecoveredPowerKW

      private double dynamicRecoveredPowerKW
      Dynamic recovered shaft power used for ramped load coupling in kW.

    • recoveredPowerRampRateKWperSec

      private double recoveredPowerRampRateKWperSec
      Maximum ramp rate for recovered shaft power in kW/s.

    • externalShaftLoadKW

      private double externalShaftLoadKW
      External shaft load attached to the expander in kW.

    • coupledCompressorLoad

      private Compressor coupledCompressorLoad
      Compressor or rotating load mechanically coupled to the expander shaft.

    • coupledCompressorSpeedRatio

      private double coupledCompressorSpeedRatio
      Speed multiplier from expander shaft speed to the coupled compressor speed.

  • Method Details

    • getExpanderMechanicalDesign

      public ExpanderMechanicalDesign getExpanderMechanicalDesign()

      expander mechanical design

    • initExpanderMechanicalDesign

      private void initExpanderMechanicalDesign()
      Initialize the expander mechanical design.

    • getRatedRecoveredPower

      public double getRatedRecoveredPower()
      Gets the rated recovered shaft power of the expander.
      Returns:
      rated recovered power in kW; 0 means no recovered-power limit is defined

    • setRatedRecoveredPower

      public void setRatedRecoveredPower(double ratedRecoveredPowerKW)
      Sets the rated recovered shaft power of the expander and rebuilds the capacity constraints so the recoveredPower utilization reflects the new rating.

      The recovered power of an expander is mechanical work extracted from the gas, so Compressor.getPower() returns a negative value. The recoveredPower constraint compares the magnitude of the extracted power against this rating, giving a meaningful 0–100+% utilization for the turbo-expander. When the rating is 0 no recovered-power constraint is created.

      Parameters:
      ratedRecoveredPowerKW - rated recovered power in kW; must be ≥ 0

    • getNozzleOpening

      public double getNozzleOpening()
      Gets the current inlet guide vane or nozzle opening.
      Returns:
      current opening fraction between the configured minimum and maximum values

    • setNozzleOpening

      public void setNozzleOpening(double opening)
      Sets the current inlet guide vane or nozzle opening.
      Parameters:
      opening - opening fraction where 0 is closed and 1 is fully open

    • getTargetNozzleOpening

      public double getTargetNozzleOpening()
      Gets the target inlet guide vane or nozzle opening.
      Returns:
      target opening fraction where 0 is closed and 1 is fully open

    • setTargetNozzleOpening

      public void setTargetNozzleOpening(double opening)
      Sets the target inlet guide vane or nozzle opening.
      Parameters:
      opening - target opening fraction where 0 is closed and 1 is fully open

    • setNozzleOpeningLimits

      public void setNozzleOpeningLimits(double minimumOpening, double maximumOpening)
      Sets the allowed inlet guide vane or nozzle opening range.

      minimumOpening - minimum opening fraction where 0 is closed and 1 is fully open
      maximumOpening - maximum opening fraction where 0 is closed and 1 is fully open

      Gets the maximum inlet guide vane or nozzle stroke rate.

      public void setNozzleOpeningRate(double nozzleOpeningRate)
      Sets the maximum inlet guide vane or nozzle stroke rate.
      Parameters:
      nozzleOpeningRate - stroke rate in opening fraction per second; negative values are treated as zero

      coupledCompressorLoad - compressor load to drive from the expander shaft; may be null

      public Compressor getCoupledCompressorLoad()
      Gets the compressor or rotating load coupled to the expander shaft.
      Returns:
      coupled compressor load, or null when no compressor is coupled

      public void setCoupledCompressorSpeedRatio(double coupledCompressorSpeedRatio)
      Sets the speed ratio from the expander shaft to the coupled compressor shaft.
      Parameters:
      coupledCompressorSpeedRatio - speed ratio; negative values are treated as zero

    • getCoupledCompressorSpeedRatio

      public double getCoupledCompressorSpeedRatio()
      Gets the speed ratio from the expander shaft to the coupled compressor shaft.
      Returns:
      coupled compressor speed ratio

    • initializeCapacityConstraints

      protected void initializeCapacityConstraints()
      Initializes default capacity constraints for this compressor.

      Creates constraints for speed, power, and surge margin based on the compressor's design parameters. Additional constraints can be added after construction.

      Builds the inherited compressor capacity constraints, then adapts them for expander operation. The consumed-power constraints (power and ratedPower) read Compressor.getPower(), which is negative for an expander and therefore always reports zero utilization, so they are removed. When a rated recovered power has been set, a recoveredPower constraint is added that tracks the magnitude of the extracted shaft power against the rating.

      Overrides:
      initializeCapacityConstraints in class Compressor

    • isSimulationValid

      public boolean isSimulationValid()
      Checks if the current simulation result is physically valid.

      Returns false if calculated values are outside physically possible ranges. This method should be overridden by specific equipment types to perform equipment-specific validation. For example:

      • Compressor: power must be positive, head must be positive
      • Heat exchanger: duty direction must match temperature change
      • Separator: phase fractions must sum to 1.0

      Validates that the compressor simulation produced physically reasonable results. Checks for:

      • Positive power consumption (compressors consume energy)
      • Positive polytropic head (work done on gas)
      • Outlet temperature greater than inlet (compression heats gas)
      • Pressure ratio greater than 1.0
      • Non-NaN values for key properties
      • Speed within compressor chart limits (if chart is active)
      • Operating point not in surge region (if surge curve is defined)
      • Operating point not beyond stonewall/choke (if stonewall curve is defined)

      Validates that the expander simulation produced physically reasonable results. Unlike a compressor, an expander extracts work from the gas, so it is valid for the shaft power to be negative, the polytropic head to be negative, and the gas to cool across the machine. This override checks only for non-NaN key values and that the outlet gas is not hotter than the inlet (within a small numerical tolerance). Using the compressor validity rules here would incorrectly flag every expander as invalid and trigger a spurious 150% utilization fallback.

      Specified by:
      isSimulationValid in interface ProcessEquipmentInterface
      Overrides:
      isSimulationValid in class Compressor
      Returns:
      true if simulation results are physically valid, false otherwise

    • runTransient

      public void runTransient(double dt, UUID id)
      runTransient This method calculates thermodynamic and unit operations using difference equations if available and calculateSteadyState is true. Use setCalculateSteadyState to set the parameter. Sets calc identifier UUID.
      Specified by:
      runTransient in interface SimulationInterface
      Overrides:
      runTransient in class Compressor
      Parameters:
      dt - Delta time [s]
      id - Calculation identifier

    • applyNozzleController

      private void applyNozzleController(double dt, UUID id)
      Applies the attached controller to the nozzle-opening target when active.
      Parameters:
      dt - transient time step in seconds
      id - calculation identifier

    • limitSpeedChange

      private double limitSpeedChange(double currentSpeedRPM, double targetSpeedRPM, double dt)
      Limits the shaft speed change using the inherited acceleration and deceleration rates.
      Parameters:
      currentSpeedRPM - current shaft speed in rpm
      targetSpeedRPM - target shaft speed in rpm from the inertia equation
      dt - transient time step in seconds
      Returns:
      limited shaft speed in rpm

      private void updateCoupledCompressor(UUID id)
      Updates a mechanically coupled compressor load with the current expander shaft speed.

      id - calculation identifier

      private double clampOpening(double opening)
      Clamps a nozzle opening to the configured physical limits.
      Parameters:
      opening - requested nozzle opening fraction
      Returns:
      clamped nozzle opening fraction

    • run

      public void run(UUID id)
      In this method all thermodynamic and unit operations will be calculated in a steady state calculation.
      Specified by:
      run in interface SimulationInterface
      Overrides:
      run in class Compressor
      Parameters:
      id - UUID
